root_text.sn35.166:1.3: “kathaṁ nu kho, bhante, jānato kathaṁ passato sakkāyadiṭṭhi pahīyatī”ti?
root_text.sn35.166:1.4: “Cakkhuṁ kho, bhikkhu, dukkhato jānato passato sakkāyadiṭṭhi pahīyati.
root_text.sn35.166:1.5: Rūpe dukkhato jānato passato sakkāyadiṭṭhi pahīyati.
root_text.sn35.166:1.6: Cakkhuviññāṇaṁ dukkhato jānato passato sakkāyadiṭṭhi pahīyati.
root_text.sn35.166:1.7: Cakkhusamphassaṁ dukkhato jānato passato sakkāyadiṭṭhi pahīyati …pe…
root_text.sn35.166:1.8: yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi dukkhato jānato passato sakkāyadiṭṭhi pahīyati.
root_text.sn35.166:1.9: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhu, jānato evaṁ passato sakkāyadiṭṭhi pahīyatī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.166:1.1: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha …
translation_text.sn35.166:1.3: “Sir, how does one know and see so that substantialist view is given up?”
translation_text.sn35.166:1.4: “Mendicant, knowing and seeing the eye, sights, eye consciousness, and eye contact as suffering, substantialist view is given up. …
translation_text.sn35.166:1.8: And also knowing and seeing the pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact as suffering, substantialist view is given up.
translation_text.sn35.166:1.9: This is how to know and see so that substantialist view is given up.”
